text.title: pytest Fixture Design
text.summary: Fixture scopes and when to use each, yield cleanup, vertical and horizontal composition, conftest layering, and autouse.

section.title: Scope Selection
section.rule: function scope (default): isolated per test. Use for stateful fixtures (DB records, file handles).
class scope: shared across methods in a test class. Use for read-only class-level setup.
module scope: shared across all tests in a file. Use for expensive read-only setup (parsed config, loaded schema).
session scope: shared for entire test run. Use ONLY for truly immutable resources (opened TCP connections, loaded models).
A wide-scope fixture cannot depend on a narrower-scope fixture — pytest raises an error.

section.title: Yield Fixtures
section.rule: Use yield instead of request.addfinalizer — cleanup is co-located and runs even if setup fails after yield.

example.code: @pytest.fixture(scope="module") def smtp_connection(): import smtplib conn = smtplib.SMTP("smtp.example.com", 587) conn.starttls() yield conn conn.quit() # guaranteed cleanup

section.title: Vertical Composition (fixture chains)
section.rule: Build higher-level fixtures from lower-level ones by declaring them as parameters.

section.title: Horizontal Composition (multiple fixtures)
section.rule: Request multiple independent fixtures in a test when they provide different concerns.

example.code: def test_checkout(authenticated_client, make_product, mailoutbox): product = make_product(price=50.0) authenticated_client.post("/checkout/", {"product_id": product.id}) assert len(mailoutbox) == 1 assert "Order confirmed" in mailoutbox[0].subject

section.title: Autouse Fixtures
section.rule: Use autouse=True only for cross-cutting side effects: clearing caches, resetting singletons, patching env vars.
Always document autouse fixtures so developers know they run implicitly.
